characters.physical_details: Lean, scar-handed, sun-weathered, short graying hair.

key_relationships.kem_aldraste: guide and manipulator, then crossing partner

characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes common script; handles Hoarded paperwork.

state_transitions.change: Heals Hael for 2 and starts north at 31 with Maren's journal.
Falls to 28 breaking the first retrieval attack.
Falls to 22 and cracks left ribs in the grain-silo fight.
Falls to 20 escaping Thessa's settlement after Thessa exposes the earlier search for a high-level defector.
Ends the Margins at 18 while Kem burns the reserve meant for the Scour.
At 14, admits she loved Maren and had been trying to follow her crossing.
Falls to 8 scouting the Scour and using the mine tunnel; learns her old threshold math is obsolete.
Spends 8 to 0 at the threshold and crosses.

characters.physical_details: Tall; starts soft from indoor life, ends lean, sunburned, bruised, and callused.
characters.occupation_status: House Aldraste heir, then zeroed arrival below.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in common and Hoarded formal script.

state_transitions.change: Leaves the Vault, spends 12, takes the signet, and ends at 288.
Falls to 245 in his first real fight.
Falls to 183 after the grain-silo perimeter.
Falls to 160 escaping Thessa's settlement and learns Vel had searched for a high-level partner.
Falls from 148 to 50 in the Margins by healing, breaking a labor wall, fighting enforcers, and repairing infrastructure.
Reaches 31 after the road-collapse rescue, learns he was recruited as threshold fuel, and pockets the signet.
Falls to 25 rescuing a family from a collapsing house and rejects preservation.
At 18, hears the full Maren story and keeps walking south on explicit terms.
Falls to 12 getting Vel through the mine tunnel and onto the basin floor.
Spends 12 to 0 through the last shield, motion spend, and final push; crosses.
Learns to live without the hum and leaves the House signet on a windowsill.

characters.availability: Present from chapter 2 onward; crosses in chapter 18.

characters.physical_details: Starts administratively preserved; ends road-worn, callused, and chapped.

key_relationships.kem_aldraste: retrieval target turned subject of sympathy

characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate; expert in Hoarded records and procedure.

state_transitions.change: Uses Wayfinder, drops to 298, and leaves the Vault with an untrained team.
At 270 after six weeks outside, lies to Lady Aldraste that she is 285.
At 238, has already filed a false natural-depletion report, sends the remaining agents back, and warns Vel and Kem about the Scour.
Uses two Perception spends to read the basin, reaches 236, and confirms that two contributors are no longer reliable.
Burns 7 to steady the threshold and ends at 229.
Keeps the official story at level-zero termination with no threshold event.

characters.availability: Present in the pursuit and at the basin; remains above the threshold.

characters.physical_details: Stocky, road-darkened trader; later carries a badly healed left-forearm gash.

key_relationships.mira: eight-year-old daughter in Roth; her legs need 40 levels of healer work; his zero

characters.occupation_status: Southern-route trader earning toward Mira's treatment.

state_transitions.change: Joins at 138 after negotiating a double eastern-run fee.
Takes a deep left-forearm wound from silo stone.
Kem stitches the arm badly but keeps it usable.
At about 130, stays beyond the original contract after watching Kem spend on strangers.
Turns back north at about 120 for Mira.

characters.occupation_status: Settlement householder and trader at level 78.

state_transitions.change: Reveals the reporting bargain that buys grain priority, timber, healer visits, and medicine.

characters.occupation_status: Former field-debt laborer at level 4 after thirty years of Hoarded development work.

state_transitions.change: Confirms that people who hit zero at the Scour can vanish without bodies; names Sera and Hoster.

characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in Hoarded formal script.

characters.physical_details: Physically perfect at 300 but mentally thinned by advanced Stillness.
characters.literacy_languages: Formerly fully literate; current cognition is degraded.

characters.physical_details: Gray at the temples, notched ear, thin chin scar, stronger forearms from manual labor below.

key_relationships.vel_ashwick: former lover reunited after eight years

characters.occupation_status: Former route explorer; later on the southern district committee for infrastructure and intake.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate; co-author of the route journal.

state_transitions.change: Reunites with Vel and receives Kem in the lower settlement.

characters.physical_details: Compact, weathered Red Reserve commander with a kept jaw scar.

key_relationships.kem_aldraste: retrieval subject she tries to turn back
key_relationships.sable_orm: auditor whose intervention turns suppression into witness

characters.occupation_status: Red Reserve commander at the Scour.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in Hoarded field procedure and reporting.

state_transitions.change: Reveals the three-year-old dampening order, three prior attempts, and the failure of the old two-person math.
Tries to keep Vel and Kem off the point, then accepts Sable's false report.

characters.physical_details: Older woman with short gray hair, old burn scars, and a green-stained apron.

key_relationships.fallow: one of the vanished Scour crossers Fallow remembers

characters.occupation_status: Intake worker and practical medic below the threshold.

state_transitions.change: Revealed alive below the threshold and receives Vel and Kem at intake.

characters.availability: Named in chapter 12; present in chapter 19.

timeline.story_start: Late spring, south of the Vault, just after Vel heals Hael and hears of Kem's defection.

seasonal_track.season: Early summer across the Spent Towns, river boundary, and first Margins settlements.
High summer across the deep Margins, the Scour, and the first day below.

key_events.event: Vel heads north with Maren's journal after healing Hael.
Kem leaves the Vault, spends 12, and takes the House signet.
Dren joins; the grain-silo fight wounds Dren and Vel and drives Kem to 183.
Thessa exposes the reporting bargain and Vel's earlier search for a high-level defector.
In the Margins, Kem spends 98 levels helping strangers and infrastructure; Fallow confirms the Scour vanishings.
Sable warns that the Scour has a permanent garrison and more Reserve may arrive.
key_events.timing: Roughly four travel days before the basin by the route taken

key_events.event: Osen reveals the site was damped three years earlier and two contributors are no longer reliable.
Vel reaches zero, Sable adds 7 levels from outside the mouth, and Kem spends his last two levels to cross.
Maren and Sera receive the arrivals below; Sable and Osen formalize the lie that protects the crossing.

geography.primary_setting: Road from the Vault through the Spent Towns and the Margins to the Scour, then first arrival in the sub-Ledger.

key_locations.name: Northern farming settlement
key_locations.details: Vel's starting point: rust-fever losses, thin labor, payment in marks or barter.

key_locations.name: The Vault
key_locations.details: Hoarded capital and seat of House Aldraste; includes the ceremonial gate and a sealed eastern wing tied to the Red Reserve.

key_locations.name: Spent Towns
key_locations.details: Settlements where routine useful spending keeps roads, farms, healing, and repairs running.

key_locations.name: Thessa's settlement
key_locations.details: Site of Thessa's bargain and a Hoarded representative already showing Stillness.

key_locations.name: River boundary
key_locations.details: Ford that marks the practical boundary between the Spent territories and the Margins.

key_locations.name: The Margins
key_locations.details: Chalk-pale depleted country of debt notices, labor compounds, and failing Shaping.

key_locations.name: The Scour
key_locations.details: Wind-scoured basin holding the damped threshold, permanent Reserve camp, mine entrance, and watched approaches.

key_locations.name: Sub-Ledger valley settlements
key_locations.details: Hand-built stone communities with intake, infirmary, terraces, cisterns, and Maren's lower settlement.

world_rules: Everyone is born at level 300. Ability use permanently spends levels, and no restoration method is established.
Ordinary labor, walking, hunger, and ordinary pain cost nothing; the Ledger prices enhanced abilities only.
Above zero, remaining levels register as a bodily hum. The sub-Ledger has no hum.
Hoarded preservation at 300 produces Stillness, and the damage continues even after a person leaves and starts spending.
Spent Town life depends on routine useful expenditure; the Margins show the same system turned into extraction and debt administration.
Level zero is not automatic death. At a true threshold it becomes passage out of the Ledger world.
Threshold strength depends on nearby spend and the size of the drop. The Scour was deliberately damped three years before the climax.
By chapter 17, two contributors are no longer reliable at the Scour. The successful crossing uses Vel's terminal spend, Kem's terminal spend, and Sable's seven-level assist from outside the mouth.
Hoarded leadership knows the threshold and sub-Ledger are real and suppresses that truth through secrecy, false reporting, the Red Reserve, and site control.
Below the threshold there are no levels and no abilities; society there runs on labor, medicine, and committee governance.

power_structure.over: Official truth, land, trade, and district policy in the Ledger world
power_structure.mechanism: Hereditary rank, law, debt records, and control of what zero is allowed to mean in public
De facto leadership while Lord Aldraste declines under Stillness

power_structure.over: Margins settlements, labor compounds, and traveler intelligence
power_structure.mechanism: Productivity tiers, ability-debt, labor reassignment, and bargains for information
Permanent garrison, trained combat spending, and site dampening

power_structure.over: Infrastructure, intake, labor, and resource disputes below the threshold
power_structure.mechanism: Committee and council decisions in a society with no Ledger

objects.name: Vel's Ledger
objects.status: Starts at 33, drops to 31 in chapter 1, and reaches 0 at the Scour in chapter 18.

objects.name: Kem's Ledger
objects.status: Starts at 300 and reaches 0 at the Scour in chapter 18.

objects.name: Kem's House signet
objects.origin: House Aldraste stand at the Vault gate
objects.status: Taken on departure, pocketed in chapter 13, left on a windowsill below in chapter 19.

objects.name: Maren's route journal
objects.origin: Shared field journal from Vel and Maren's route years
objects.status: Main route source; contains Scour notes, basin sketch, and the threshold mark.

objects.name: Margins productivity notices
objects.origin: District notice board in the Margins
objects.status: Pulled down in chapter 12 and carried south as evidence of the debt regime.

debts.amount: Forty levels' worth of bone-work for Mira's legs; the previous payment covered about half
debts.deadline: Ongoing; Dren turns back in chapter 16 to keep earning toward it

debts.amount: Margins tier debt: Tier 1 owes 0.3 levels per year, Tier 2 owes 0.15, Tier 3 can be reassigned as Renewable Expenditure Units, Tier 4 faces account closure, and appeals cost 2 levels
debts.deadline: Recurring district review and labor reassignment cycles
debts.status: Systemic debt structure imposed on Margins residents

income_sources.source: Vel's freelance healing and repair work
income_sources.amount: Small payments in marks or barter
Run-by-run trader income; a southern guide fee is roughly a double eastern run
income_sources.reliability: Reliable when he can stay on route

income_sources.amount: Finite silver fittings, clasps, and other portable value converted into marks on the road
Grain priority, timber allotments, healer visits twice a season, and lung-fever medicine
income_sources.reliability: Reliable only while she keeps supplying route intelligence

knowledge_state.knows: Maren crossed at the Scour eight years earlier; zero at the right threshold is passage, not ordinary death; Vel hid the journal's real purpose and her search for a high-level partner until the trip south forced disclosure. By chapter 17 she also knows the site was damped and her old two-person math is obsolete.
knowledge_state.learned_in: Eight years before story start; partially disclosed in chapters 09, 13, and 16; corrected in chapter_17
hidden_from: kem_aldraste until chapters 09, 13, and 16 depending on the part of the truth
most other characters for most of the book

knowledge_state.knows: Stillness is damage rather than virtue; by chapter 15 he knows it follows people outside the Vault; by chapter 16 he knows Vel recruited him for the threshold; by chapter 19 he knows the vanished survive below.
knowledge_state.learned_in: Before story start; expanded in chapters 15, 16, and 19

knowledge_state.knows: The Hoarded bought traveler intelligence from her, and Vel had already been asking after high-level defectors years before Kem.
The Red Reserve exists, the Scour has a permanent garrison, the site was damped, and the official cover story after the crossing is level-zero termination with no threshold event.
knowledge_state.learned_in: chapter_06 onward; dampening file retrieved before chapter_17

knowledge_state.knows: The current Scour arithmetic, the dampening order, and three prior attempts at the threshold.
knowledge_state.learned_in: Five years of permanent station and classified reporting before chapter_17

knowledge_state.knows: People who hit zero at the Scour can vanish with no body left behind; Sera and Hoster are named examples.
knowledge_state.learned_in: Observed over decades in the deep Margins
hidden_from: largely unknown outside the deep Margins

knowledge_state.knows: The threshold and sub-Ledger are real and worth suppressing.
hidden_from: most people in the Spent Towns and the Margins

environmental_constants: Above zero, levels feel like a background hum; its absence marks the sub-Ledger immediately.
The Vault is controlled, quiet, and built to make stillness feel virtuous.
Spent Towns sound alive with routine productive spending.
The Margins are dry, chalk-pale, overused, and bureaucratically scarred.
The Scour is exposed wind, layered stone, heat, and very little cover.
Below the threshold, every surface shows hand labor: tool-marked stone, terraces, cisterns, and obsidian medicine.
